title: Brown Lentil Scramble
description: A salty and crumbly British-inspired dish using brown lentils as a base for a vegan-friendly breakfast option.

ingredients.name: Optional: 2 tablespoons fresh dill
ingredients.notes: Chopped for garnish (for an extra touch of freshness) - not essential

instructions: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Rinse the brown lentils under cold water and pick out any debris. Set aside.

In a medium saucepan, combine the rinsed lentils with the water or vegetable broth. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce heat to low and let simmer for about 20-25 minutes or until the lentils are tender but still have some bite.
While the lentils are cooking, in a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and chopped onion, sautéing until softened and fragrant, approximately 3-4 minutes.

Drain the lentils from their liquid, reserving any remaining broth for later use if needed. Return the cooked lentils to the saucepan along with the drained ones, add salt and pepper to taste, and mix well. Add a splash of reserved broth if the mixture is too thick.
Transfer the lentil mixture into the skillet with the sautéed onion and garlic. Allow everything to warm through for about 5-7 minutes.

To serve, you can stir in fresh dill (if using) just before serving. Garnish with extra dill or a sprinkle of paprika if desired. Serve hot as is, or drizzle with a bit of olive oil and additional fresh herbs for added flavor.
Optional: For an egg-free version, allow the lentils to cool slightly, then blend them in a blender until smooth. This can be used as a base for scrambled tofu or tempeh if desired.
